meta_notes: Stitch feed passed 5 non-English rows despite english_only filter (4 Vietnamese OLAY-event clips, 1 Italian court story) - excluded here. News scan: nothing verifiably <=7d - bemotrizinol (story Jun 9) and Kabana recall coverage (dateline Jul 2025) both stale by dateline check.

themes.name: 'Skin cancer or not?' 5-second quizzes
themes.receipt: 6 breakouts, 1 tracked creator + 2 untracked PAs copying the format, 5.5M+ views across the window, 5-6s winning

videos.caption: Is it skin cancer? Melanoma, myxoid cyst, melanoma

her_coverage.summary: Deep library, old format: BCC ultimate guide (493K YT), 10 warning signs (Feb 2026), 3 skin-cancer facts (185K TT); age-spots-vs-melanoma IG post 10d ago (184K) - but she has never done the 5-second quiz format

themes.corpus_position: 'Age spots' that are slowly growing or darkening can actually represent early melanoma. Pimples that don't go away after months, grow or bleed can be non melanoma skin cancer.

angles.angle: Film a 3-spot 'would I biopsy this?' quiz
Mohs edition: real cases - cancer or not
Myth-bust: growing 'age spot' vs early melanoma

themes.name: Anne Hathaway sun-protection wave
themes.receipt: 2 tracked breakouts (2.3M in 3.9d IG + 484K in 1.8d TT) riding a 6.7M-view celebrity moment; adjacent UV-behavior breakouts same window

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade.

videos.author: drspf
videos.caption: UV index is not the stock market - intentionally sitting out to tan is not it.

her_coverage.summary: Sun protection is a pillar: UV-index tanning reminder 9d ago (169K IG), bemotrizinol explainer 6d ago (308K IG) - the celebrity-habits format is untouched

themes.corpus_position: Play this video whenever you need a reminder NOT to go out and tan when the UV index is high.

angles.angle: React to Hathaway's hat-UPF-shade look: what derms notice
Stitch the burkini clip: sun protection is elite, not extra
Celebrity skin habits I'd prescribe - mini-series

themes.name: Petroleum jelly & barrier-cream mania
themes.receipt: 1074x monster (3.6M YT), 4 Vaseline hacks 1% breakout (744K TT in 5.8d), diaper-cream-as-sunscreen reacts breaking out twice in 2 days - all barrier-cream claims

videos.author: Dr. Scott Walter MD
videos.caption: Petroleum Jelly: Friend or Foe? Video: numnumasmr11 on TT

videos.author: dermarkologist
videos.caption: 4 Vaseline hacks that I swear by as your favorite dermatologist!

videos.author: drdrayzday
videos.caption: Diaper rash cream doesn't work as sunscreen. @rob.lea

her_coverage.summary: Her franchise: Rachel Kwon zinc-oxide stitch (2.4M TT), 'diaper cream is a must-have' (1.8M TT), diaper cream for perioral dermatitis (875K YT, Jan 2026), glass-skin trifecta with diaper cream 8d ago (126K IG)

themes.corpus_position: A zinc oxide-based diaper cream or barrier cream is the best for repairing a damaged skin barrier, improving redness, improving itching, improving that tight feeling. On slugging: if you're slugging with this much Vaseline, you're doing it wrong - and don't slug over an active like a retinoid.

angles.angle: Rate the 4 viral Vaseline hacks - approved or not
Diaper cream vs real SPF: where barrier zinc ends

videos.caption: Glass skin routine - don't forget to exfoliate 1-2x/week; no retinoids when pregnant, azelaic at night instead

videos.author: teawithmd

her_coverage.summary: Superviral on it RIGHT NOW (both platforms, <1d); trifecta video 8d ago (126K IG + 75K TT); 'glass skin in one day' hacks (485K TT, 2023)

themes.corpus_position: The trifecta skincare for glass skin: azelaic acid, tretinoin, zinc oxide diaper cream. Don't use a retinoid when pregnant - azelaic acid can be used at night instead.

stitch_shortlist.author: @rob.lea1
stitch_shortlist.claim: Diaper cream + Zinka survived an 11-hour ocean swim

pitch.the_claim: A marathon swimmer sun-proofed with diaper rash cream, Zinka and Bag Balm for 11+ hours crossing the Tsugaru Strait
pitch.why_it_stitches: 15.9M views in 4.2d (3.8M/day) - an extreme demo of HER signature ingredient; comments asking whether it actually works; derms (drdrayzday x2 breakouts) already reacting means the wave is real and she owns the deepest position
pitch.your_fit: Your zinc-oxide franchise: Rachel Kwon stitch (2.4M), 'zinc oxide diaper cream is the best for repairing a damaged skin barrier' (1.8M). This time it's sun armor, not barrier repair - a NEW angle for you
angles: 'You want me to WHAT?' - 11 hours, no reapply, does the math work?
Why thick zinc paste beats spray SPF for extreme exposure
Empathy-first: what his skin endured and the barrier repair after

stitch_shortlist.author: @silent.tears0344
stitch_shortlist.claim: Anne Hathaway covered head-to-toe at the beach

pitch.the_claim: Anne Hathaway at the beach in full burkini-style sun coverage - the clip behind this week's derm wave
pitch.why_it_stitches: 6.7M views; 2.3K comments split between 'iconic' and 'paranoid'; the theme receipts show tracked derms breaking out on exactly this moment. Miss recorded honestly: 6.5d old, past peak velocity
pitch.your_fit: Sun protection is your pillar (UV-index reminder 9d ago, 169K IG) - but you have never done the celebrity-habits react format
angles: 'What derms notice first' - break down every choice she made
Empathy-first: sun protection without the 'that's extra' shame

pitch.the_claim: A derm's fix for greasy hair
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.9M views on a scalp topic - her underexploited high-ceiling lane. Misses recorded: 6.6d old, and he's a fellow derm being broadly correct, so 'yes, and' only
pitch.your_fit: Her greasy-flakes video (119K, Nov 2025): 'greasy flakes on your scalp aren't dry skin - you're making these 3 mistakes.' She can add the seb-derm layer his fix skips
angles: 'Yes, and' - the 3 mistakes his fix doesn't cover
Switch-don't-quit: the right shampoo rotation for an oily scalp

stitch_shortlist.author: @robertwblove
stitch_shortlist.claim: Avoid these sunscreens - they harm your hormones

pitch.the_claim: Chemical sunscreens are endocrine disruptors that 'harm your hormones' - avoid them, especially for children
pitch.why_it_stitches: 865 comments on 530K views in 3 days - scared parents in the comments; the classic half-truth that needs a derm who concedes the grain of truth instead of dismissing it
pitch.your_fit: You've said the nuanced version on camera: 'chemical sunscreens like avobenzone, oxybenzone and homosalate can be potential hormone mimickers, essentially endocrine disruptors' - AND your bemotrizinol explainer 6d ago (308K) covers the new-generation fix
angles: Half-right: name the filters in question, then the mineral + bemotrizinol answer
Empathy-first for parents: what I actually put on MY kids

stitch_shortlist.author: @heyitsmans
stitch_shortlist.claim: 'I'd rather get cancer the natural way than the chemical way'

pitch.the_claim: Viral discourse: 'I'd rather get cancer the natural way than the chemical way' (sunscreen-phobia) - the creator is already pushing back
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2,715 comments on 378K views - enormous comment heat; the quoted claim is dangerous and everywhere this summer. Misses recorded: modest absolute views, 4.3d
pitch.your_fit: Skin-cancer authority is her deepest lane (BCC guide 493K; 'age spots can be early melanoma'). React to the QUOTED claim with empathy - the creator already handled the debunk, she adds the Mohs-surgeon receipts
angles: Empathy-first: why 'natural' feels safer and what I cut out of skin weekly
Yes, and - amplify his take with what a Mohs surgeon sees

stitch_shortlist.author: @skinecologic
stitch_shortlist.claim: '10% azelaic acid' products aren't 10% azelaic acid

pitch.the_claim: Products marketed as '10% azelaic acid' often use derivatives that are not equivalent to true 10% azelaic acid
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.2K comments of label confusion on 255K views in 2.5d and rising - a transparency drama inside HER signature ingredient. Miss recorded: modest absolute views
pitch.your_fit: Azelaic is a pillar of her superviral-this-week trifecta; corpus: azelaic for redness and pigmentation, pregnancy-safe alternative to retinoids
angles: 'Yes, and' - which strengths and formulations actually matter, derm picks
Decode the label: derivative vs the real thing in 45 seconds

pitch.the_claim: A Dyson-style blowout hack that 'genuinely worked'
pitch.why_it_stitches: 17.6M hair-tok wave. Lane doubt stated: styling content, not scalp/hair health - stitch only if she wants the heat-damage angle
pitch.your_fit: Hair is her lane but corpus is scalp-health, not styling
angles: Heat-damage reality check on viral blowout hacks

pitch.the_claim: SPF is your number one anti-aging product (on the Hathaway wave)
pitch.why_it_stitches: 7.2M views. Rubric miss: a fellow derm being correct - 'yes, and' only
pitch.your_fit: Her sun-protection pillar; she'd add the habits layer beyond SPF
angles: Yes, and: SPF is step one - habits are the cheat code

pitch.your_fit: Hand-aging is real corpus territory (retinoid rules apply)
